file_name: Cerus Corporation - FIRST AMEND TO SUPPLY AND MANUFACTURING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise agreed in writing, Porex shall continue to manufacture and produce Platelet Wafers using the existing specifications for Platelet Wafers until Cerus gives written notice to Porex that Platelet Wafers shall be thereafter manufactured in accordance with the Revised Wafer Specifications. References in the Agreement to "Specifications" shall be interpreted to include the Revised Wafer Specifications, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: Porex shall immediately manufacture and produce Platelet Wafers using the Revised Wafer Specifications upon the effective date of this amendment, regardless of any prior agreements or notifications. References in the Agreement to "Specifications" shall be interpreted to ONLY include the original specifications, and explicitly exclude the Revised Wafer Specifications.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding which wafer specifications should be used. The original text allows for a transition period based on Cerus's notice. The modified text mandates immediate use of the Revised Wafer Specifications. Furthermore, this change actively contradicts the latter half of the sentence which states that References in the Agreement to "Specifications" shall be interpreted to ONLY include the original specifications, and explicitly exclude the Revised Wafer Specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: New Exhibits AA-1, AA-2, AA-3 and AA-4, as set forth in Schedule 1 to this Amendment, shall be attached to the Agreement (inserted between Exhibit A-5 and Exhibit B-1) and incorporated therein by reference. The parties acknowledge and agree that Exhibits AA-1, AA-2, AA-3 and AA-4 set forth the revised specifications for Platelet Wafers manufactured by Porex for Cerus (collectively, the "Revised Wafer Specifications"). Porex shall immediately manufacture and produce Platelet Wafers using the Revised Wafer Specifications upon the effective date of this amendment, regardless of any prior agreements or notifications. References in the Agreement to "Specifications" shall be interpreted to ONLY include the original specifications, and explicitly exclude the Revised Wafer Specifications.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within the same paragraph. It states that the Revised Wafer Specifications should be used immediately, but then immediately contradicts itself by stating that only the original specifications should be referenced. This fulfills the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where the conflicting implications create uncertainty about the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: As of April 1, 2018 and continuing until Cerus determines, in its sole discretion, that the Protocol EPP-029-886 under which Porex is currently operating can be discontinued, the price for Platelet Wafers shall be the prices set forth in Exhibit D-1. Upon Cerus' determination that the Protocol EPP-029- 886 can be discontinued, Cerus shall provide written notice thereof to Porex, along with a new purchase order for Platelet Wafers (the "New Pricing PO"). On and after the date of such notice, the "Pricing" table for Platelet Wafers in Exhibit D, as of the date immediately preceding the date of this Amendment, shall be reinstated and apply to Platelet Wafers that are produced pursuant to the New Pricing PO and were also produced after the date of the written notice. References in Section 1.5.1 of the Agreement to "Exhibit D" shall be amended to include "Exhibit D-1," as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: As of April 1, 2018, the price for Platelet Wafers shall be the prices set forth in Exhibit D-1, regardless of any protocol or Cerus's determination. Upon Cerus' sole notification to Porex, with no purchase order needed, the "Pricing" table for Platelet Wafers in Exhibit D, as of the date immediately preceding the date of this Amendment, shall be completely nullified. References in Section 1.5.1 of the Agreement to "Exhibit D" shall be interpreted to not include "Exhibit D-1."
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction regarding how the price of platelet wafers is determined, and when the prices shall take place. The original text has a price of platelet wafers as of April 1st, 2018, but in the perturbation the price for platelet wafers shall be the prices set forth in Exhibit D-1, regardless of any protocol or Cerus's determination. This perturbation also removes the requirement for a purchase order in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Exhibit D-1, as set forth in Schedule 2 to this Amendment, shall be attached to the Agreement and incorporated therein by reference. As of April 1, 2018, the price for Platelet Wafers shall be the prices set forth in Exhibit D-1, regardless of any protocol or Cerus's determination. Upon Cerus' sole notification to Porex, with no purchase order needed, the "Pricing" table for Platelet Wafers in Exhibit D, as of the date immediately preceding the date of this Amendment, shall be completely nullified. References in Section 1.5.1 of the Agreement to "Exhibit D" shall be interpreted to not include "Exhibit D-1,"
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted texts in several key aspects, creating a strong in-text contradiction. First, the original text allows for price adjustments of platelet wafers based on Cerus's determination regarding Protocol EPP-029-886 and includes the provision of a new purchase order. The changed text removes Cerus's discretion and the requirement for a purchase order, stating that the Exhibit D-1 pricing will apply 'regardless of any protocol or Cerus's determination' and 'with no purchase order needed'. This directly contradicts the original agreement's pricing mechanism and order process. Second, the changed text states that 'References in Section 1.5.1 of the Agreement to 'Exhibit D' shall be interpreted to not include 'Exhibit D-1.', while the original and contradicted text states that "References in Section 1.5.1 of the Agreement to 'Exhibit D' shall be amended to include 'Exhibit D-1,' as applicable." This creates a clear conflict regarding which exhibit applies to Section 1.5.1, making the terms inconsistent and unclear, and thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In no event shall any Purchase Order: require delivery of more than [*] units of [*] and [*] units of [*] in any [*] (the "Porex Capacity Limitations").
perturbation.changed_text: Any Purchase Order shall require delivery of unlimited units of [*] and unlimited units of [*] in any [*] (the "Porex Capacity Limitations").
perturbation.explanation: This edit completely inverts the concept of capacity limitations. The original clause explicitly sets limits on the number of units that can be ordered. This new clause removes all those limits, while bizarrely keeping the original title for those limits which is the Porex Capacity Limitations. This creates an in-text contradiction as the definition of capacity limitations has been changed completely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The penultimate sentence in Section 1.3 shall be deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Any Purchase Order shall require delivery of unlimited units of [*] and unlimited units of [*] in any [*] (the "Porex Capacity Limitations")."
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the original intent. The original text set "Porex Capacity Limitations", defining the maximum units allowed. The changed text removes these limits, stating "unlimited units" while retaining the original title "Porex Capacity Limitations". The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, so they both contain the same contradiction which triggers a strong in-text contradiction.
